meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code. A technical checklist for students to overcome roadblocks and finish high-impact SaaS projects.
content.answer_block: Stop letting bugs kill your momentum. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for iterative CLI-based debugging, you transform roadblocks into learning opportunities. This workflow automates the grunt work, allowing you to focus on high-level logic while ensuring your code remains production-ready and scalable for your portfolio.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the cursor blinking like a countdown to another abandoned project. You’ve spent three days fighting a dependency hell or a state management race condition that refuses to die. The dream of shipping a polished SaaS app feels like it’s slipping away, replaced by the crushing weight of 'unfinished' status on your GitHub profile.

It’s time to stop treating your code as an adversary and start treating your AI tools as a senior engineering mentor. By mastering the synergy between Antigravity’s structural insights and Claude Code’s command-line precision, you bridge the gap between 'student code' and professional-grade software. This isn't just about fixing a bug; it's about building the resilience to ship.

steps.title: Architectural Health Audit
steps.description: Before fixing, map the failure. Use Antigravity to visualize how your data flows and identify the exact layer where the logic breakdown occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase structure. Identify potential race conditions and suggest a more robust state management pattern that avoids the current bottleneck.

steps.title: Automated Error Isolation
steps.description: Feed your stack trace into Claude Code to isolate the specific module causing the crash, preventing you from refactoring code that isn't broken.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this stack trace. Which module is the primary root cause and what specific line of code is violating the interface contract?

steps.title: Test-Driven Patching
steps.description: Don't patch in production. Force the AI to write a failing test case first, then generate the fix to satisfy that test.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest unit test that reproduces the bug I'm facing. Ensure it covers the edge case I identified in the logs.

steps.title: Context-Aware Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix across the codebase, ensuring consistency. Avoid manual updates that introduce secondary bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply the fix identified in Step 3. Ensure the refactor maintains compatibility with the existing middleware across all project routes.

steps.title: Post-Mortem Documentation
steps.description: Document why the bug happened so you can explain it in job interviews. AI can synthesize your struggle into a professional technical post-mortem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a brief 'Lessons Learned' summary of this bug, explaining the root cause and the architectural change we made to prevent future regressions.

pro_tips: Always run your AI prompts with a 'Role-play' prefix like 'Act as a Senior Staff Engineer' to force higher-quality code suggestions.
Use Claude Code to automate the creation of git branches for every fix; never commit directly to main when debugging.
If the AI provides a solution you don't understand, ask it to 'Explain this logic as if I were a junior dev' to ensure you're actually learning.
Leverage the --dry-run flag in CLI tools to see the proposed file changes before committing any AI-generated patches.
Maintain a 'Bug-Book' markdown file where you log every complex issue solved; this becomes your ultimate secret weapon for technical interviews.
content.next_steps: Once your project is stable, move to deploying your MVP on a platform like Vercel or Railway and document your deployment process for your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the roadmap to break through the wall of stagnation. Stop being a student who starts, and start being an engineer who ships. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
